records.ety: [OE. ajoinen, OF. ajoindre, F. adjoindre, fr. L. adjungere; ad + jungere to join. See Join, and cf. Adjunct.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Adjoined (<?/); p. pr. & vb. n. Adjoining.]

defs: To join or unite to; to lie contiguous to; to be in contact with; to attach; to append.
quotes: Corrections . . . should be, as remarks, adjoined by way of note.

defs: To lie or be next, or in contact; to be contiguous; as, the houses adjoin.
quotes: When one man's land adjoins to another's.
notes: ☞ The construction with to, on, or with is obsolete or obsolescent.

quotes: She lightly unto him adjoined side to side.
